steps.title: Identify the subject and temporal signal
steps.content: The subject is "her painting" (singular). The adverb "Currently" signals that the verb must be in the present tense.

steps.title: Apply subject-verb agreement and present tense
steps.content:
A singular subject with a present-time signal needs a singular present-tense verb.

A) hung — simple past; contradicts "Currently." Wrong.
B) hangs — singular present tense; agrees with "her painting" and matches "Currently." Correct.
C) was hanging — past progressive; contradicts "Currently." Wrong.
D) will have hung — future perfect; contradicts "Currently." Wrong.

B is correct.
